subject: UX & Research
title: Research with Aging Populations
description: A micro-lesson that makes Research with Aging Populations usable.

hook.text: By 2050, one in six people globally will be over 65. Most digital products are designed by and for 25-year-olds.

buildup.text: Aging reduces contrast sensitivity, fine motor control, and working memory. Small text and tiny buttons become barriers.

discovery.text: Research at MIT found that touch targets need to be at least 44px for older users to tap accurately on mobile.

twist.text: Designing for older users improves products for everyone. Larger text and clearer contrast help all ages.

climax.text: Inclusive design isn't charity. It's smart business. The 65+ age group controls most of the world's wealth.

punchline.text: Design for aging eyes and shaky hands. Everyone benefits.

quiz.question: Why is designing for older users good for all users?
options: Accommodations like larger text and clearer contrast improve usability for every age group
Older users spend more money on apps
Regulations require all products to be accessible
